webLinks.linkText: Permanent changes on the Warringah Freeway

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip on public transport.
properties.otherAdvice:
Changes to traffic conditions will be in place at various times southbound along the Warringah Fwy and other nearby roads as a result of the Warringah Freeway upgrade and Western Harbour Tunnel projects.

Changes to traffic conditions are also in place for northbound motorists - Warringah Fwy northbound.

Major changes have been implemented to the Warringah Freeway to make it safer and easier to use. This may affect which lane you need to be in or the ramp you need to take. These changes include:

  • removal of the slip lane to Sydney Harbour Bridge
  • removal of access to Sydney Harbour Bridge from Ernest Street
  • change to ramp arrangements at Falcon Street/Military Road and removal of access to Cahill Expressway
  • opening of the new dedicated southbound bus lane along the Warringah Freeway
  • changes for overheight vehicles.

JERVIS BAY ROAD INTERSECTION UPGRADE

Construction work is underway, including major earthworks and embankment construction; bridge works and retaining wall construction; road surface and ramp construction; utility relocations; and drainage upgrades.

Changes to traffic conditions - MAJOR TRAFFIC SWITCH

At night (8pm to 6am), on Wed 3 & Thu 4 June, motorists are advised to allow approximately 15 minutes of additional travel time

During the day, lane and shoulder closures will be in place at various times along the Princes Hwy and other nearby roads as a result of the Jervis Bay intersection upgrade project. Motorists are advised to allow approximately 5 minutes of additional travel time when traffic control is active.

From Wednesday 3 June (Stage 1) - southbound traffic will travel along the new bridge over Jervis Bay Rd and utilise the southbound on and off ramps to the highway.

From Friday 5 June (Stage 2) - northbound traffic will also travel along the new bridge over Jervis Bay Rd. Northbound traffic wishing to access the Bay and Basin area can use Island Point Rd, Hawken Rd, or the U-turn facility at Comberton Grange Rd.

properties.otherAdvice:
Transport for NSW is proactively working to increase the durability and resilience of Chifley Rd.

Night work will include heavy patching, re-laying asphalt, and refreshing line markings. This work will take place in sections, moving along the road each shift. This work will require a reduced speed limit and alternating (stop/slow) traffic conditions to allow motorists to pass in one direction at a time. These works are likely to cause delays of up to 5 minutes.

Day work will involve widening road shoulders to improve existing pull-over bays and, where possible, create wider shoulders. This work will require a reduced speed limit and shoulder closures only.

Motorists using the Great Western Highway detour via Darling Causeway and Chifley Road are advised to allow extra travel time. If you are travelling during peak periods (Monday - Thursday 8:30am - 10am and 2pm - 3:30pm and Friday 8:30am - 3:30pm), you may need to allow around an additional hour of travel time. 

Please exercise extra caution and follow the directions of signage and traffic controllers.

There will be no work on Saturday nights or public holidays.
